## [00:00 - 01:33]

This is the Pubcast Shot. I'm John Loomer. And today we're going to talk about Meta's Twitter competitor. Let's go. Music You'd be foolish to ignore Meta's Twitter competitor. Rumors are that Barcelona, or whatever it is it's going to be called, is set to launch in June. While Meta's track record isn't especially good when it comes to copying competitors, there are reasons to think that this might be different. Normally, Meta's trying to take advantage of some new hot app or format. The problem's been that most people don't want to leave that new hot app. This, though, is the opposite. Twitter is not hot. It does have adoption, and people who are comfortable with the format. And Twitter doesn't really have a similar competitor. People have left Twitter, and others probably would if there was a decent alternative. In this case, Meta's actually trying to take advantage of a need. When people who are unhappy with the direction of Twitter be okay with a Meta-owned alternative? Tough to say. But there is potential here. And for advertisers, this could also mean the long-term potential for more ad inventory. Maybe it'll bomb. Maybe Meta'll absorb some of the features going forward. That's going to be interesting.
